channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Amid wars and soaring energy prices, IMF says the global economy is on the brink of recession
item.description: A report from the International Monetary Fund warns "the global economy is threatened with being thrown off course." European Union Commissioner Marta Kos discusses this grim economic outlook.
item.content:encoded: A report from the International Monetary Fund warns "the global economy is threatened with being thrown off course." European Union Commissioner Marta Kos discusses this grim economic outlook.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Photos: How overfishing in Southeast Asia is an ecological and human crisis
item.description: A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contested.
item.content:encoded:
A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contested.

(Image credit: Nicole Tung)
item.dc:creator: Nicole Tung

item.title: Why are oil prices affecting the U.S. if we are a net oil exporter?
item.description: The U.S. produces more oil and gas than any country in the world. So why are prices at the pump 30% higher than they were last year? The Indicator (and brand new Indicator newsletter) explains!
item.content:encoded: The U.S. produces more oil and gas than any country in the world. So why are prices at the pump 30% higher than they were last year? The Indicator (and brand new Indicator newsletter) explains!
item.dc:creator: Cooper Katz McKim

item.title: Once again, Trump threatens to fire Fed Chair Jerome Powell
item.description: President Trump once again threatened to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and doubled down on a discredited probe of the central bank.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump once again threatened to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and doubled down on a discredited probe of the central bank.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Fuel protests have Ireland's government facing possible no-confidence vote
item.description: The prime minister announced new tax cuts to try to end the crisis that began after the U.S.-Israel war on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The government could face a no-confidence vote over its response to the fuel protests.
item.content:encoded:
The prime minister announced new tax cuts to try to end the crisis that began after the U.S.-Israel war on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The government could face a no-confidence vote over its response to the fuel protests.

(Image credit: Peter Morrison)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The labor economics of 'Alien' — and its lessons for inequality on Earth
item.description: Behind the acid blood and jump scares of the Alien franchise is an even more insidious horror: a single employer with unchecked power. How Weyland-Yutani helps explain monopsony — and the rise of inequality on Earth.
item.content:encoded: Behind the acid blood and jump scares of the Alien franchise is an even more insidious horror: a single employer with unchecked power. How Weyland-Yutani helps explain monopsony — and the rise of inequality on Earth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Americans are getting bigger tax refunds, but few are noticing the benefit
item.description: Republicans have been banking on a big tax refund season as part of their Big Beautiful Bill Act. But even with bigger refunds, few Americans believe the tax changes benefit them.
item.content:encoded: Republicans have been banking on a big tax refund season as part of their Big Beautiful Bill Act. But even with bigger refunds, few Americans believe the tax changes benefit them.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Families across the U.S. are getting college acceptance letters — and tuition bills
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks with economist Judith Scott-Clayton about the cost of college in the U.S. They discuss the difference between sticker and net price and the opaqueness of tuition costs.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks with economist Judith Scott-Clayton about the cost of college in the U.S. They discuss the difference between sticker and net price and the opaqueness of tuition costs.

item.title: How farmers are dealing with the fallout from the Iran war
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe checks in Josh Gackle, former chairman of the American Soybean Association, on the state of the farming industry during the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe checks in Josh Gackle, former chairman of the American Soybean Association, on the state of the farming industry during the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: How the war in Iran is reshaping investor perceptions of Trump policies
item.description: Investors thought they had President Trump figured out as someone who would always back down from his most extreme policies. The war with Iran is challenging that.
item.content:encoded: Investors thought they had President Trump figured out as someone who would always back down from his most extreme policies. The war with Iran is challenging that.
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan
